rationale: All 3 of my round-1 items are cleanly ADDRESSED, verified by code trace + `go test -count=1 ./internal/executor/... ./internal/gitutil/... ./internal/approve/... ./internal/workspace/... ./cmd/mindspec/...` (all green). The always-pushes comment/behavior contradiction is fixed by the Group-3 reorder (mindspec_executor.go:436-443, unconditional first push) and pinned by TestFinalizeEpic_OrphanChoreFailureStillPushesSpecBranch. The self-heal (stat->WorktreeRemoveForce->os.RemoveAll->WorktreePrune, lines 598-609) is correct and pinned by TestFinalizeEpic_OrphanedSpecBranch_LeftoverTempWorktreeSelfHeals; I also checked the path-traversal angle the brief raised — wtPath is safe because specID must pass idvalidate.SpecID (no '/', no '..') at ApproveImpl, the sole production entry point, before FinalizeEpic is ever reached. TestFinalizeEpic_OrphanFetchFailureFallsBackToBaseline closes my coverage gap exactly as asked. Deviations A-C are all reasonable engineering judgment. But adversarially attacking the NEW surface as instructed, I found a real, reproducible defect: RemoteHeadSHA's ls-remote query is a suffix-pattern match, not exact-match, and `fields := strings.Fields(line)` flattens multi-line output, silently returning the wrong SHA when >1 ref matches (empirically reproduced with real git). Fail-safe today (a wrong SHA just fails the lease) and unreachable in mindspec's own repo given specID's regex format, but untested and cheap to harden. Also flagging that PushBranchForceWithLease's doc comment overclaims 'never silently clobbered' — true only for the TOCTOU window, not for a human's PRE-existing push to the open chore-branch PR, which the design will unconditionally overwrite.
concrete_changes_required: internal/gitutil/gitops.go, RemoteHeadSHA (lines 327-345): `git ls-remote --heads <remote> <branch>` does path-component SUFFIX matching, not exact-name matching — I reproduced live that `git ls-remote --heads origin chore/finalize-105` returns TWO lines when a ref like `refs/heads/aaa/chore/finalize-105` also exists (an unrelated ref whose trailing components happen to equal the query), and that the alphabetically-earlier decoy's SHA sorts FIRST in ls-remote's output. `line := strings.TrimSpace(string(out)); fields := strings.Fields(line); return fields[0]` collapses multi-line output (Fields splits on embedded newlines too) and silently returns whichever record came first — not necessarily `refs/heads/<branch>`'s own SHA — with no error and no count check. Harden: iterate the output lines, require exactly one whose second field equals the literal `"refs/heads/"+branch`, and return an error (not a best-guess SHA) if zero-with-nonempty-output or more than one match. Add a test pinning the multi-match case (currently TestRemoteHeadSHA_ParsesLsRemote only covers single-match / absent / git-error). Not exploitable in mindspec's own repo today (idvalidate.SpecID locks specID to `^[0-9]{3,}-slug$`, so `chore/finalize-<specID>` can't collide under current branch-naming conventions), and the failure mode is fail-safe (a wrong SHA just fails the lease, reproducing a 'retry hard-fails' UX regression rather than corrupting anything) — but it's real, general-purpose gitutil code with a surprising silent-wrong-answer failure mode, worth closing now while it's cheap.

internal/gitutil/gitops.go PushBranchForceWithLease doc comment (lines 348-357) and internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go finalizeOrphanedSpecBranch's push-block comment (around line ~640, 'Present (a prior run's push) -> overwrite it'): both assert or assume the observed remote tip is always 'a prior run's push' and that the lease means a moved tip 'is never silently clobbered.' That is only true for movement DURING this process's own observe-then-push pair (the true TOCTOU window) — verified correct and tested (TestPushBranchForceWithLease_ArgvAndLease's stale-info case). It is NOT true for a tip that was ALREADY there before this invocation's RemoteHeadSHA probe: if a human pushed a review-fixup commit directly to the open chore/finalize-<specID> PR (the very workflow implApproveTail's own NOTE instructs the operator into, via `gh pr create --head chore/finalize-<specID>`) at any point before a later, detached retry of `impl approve` for the same spec, RemoteHeadSHA correctly observes that human commit as the current tip, the lease is pinned to exactly that SHA, and the force push SUCCEEDS in discarding it — because the design intentionally treats the branch as always-regenerate-from-scratch, with no way to distinguish a machine-authored tip from a human-authored one. Not a regression (pre-fix, a retry just hard-failed instead) and not blocking this round, but the doc comments should be corrected to scope the 'never silently clobbered' claim to the TOCTOU window only, and this blind spot should be named explicitly — parallel to how round 1's Group 5 squash-merge blind spot was handled (comment + follow-up bead, not a functional fix required in this round).

findings.detail: gitutil.PushBranch(specBranch) now runs first and unconditionally inside the `if gitutil.HasRemote()` block (mindspec_executor.go:436-443), before orphan detection/the chore flow, with a comment explaining the Group-3 ordering rationale. The stale duplicate push + its now-false 'also survives locally' comment were removed from after the orphan flow. New test TestFinalizeEpic_OrphanChoreFailureStillPushesSpecBranch drives an export failure inside finalizeOrphanedSpecBranch and asserts (a) FinalizeEpic still errors, naming the chore branch, and (b) origin's spec/077-test tip already reflects the baseline push at its pre-finalize hash. Ran the suite: green.

findings.item: 2. Leftover temp worktree from a crashed prior run
findings.detail: finalizeOrphanedSpecBranch now self-heals BEFORE any branch operation (mindspec_executor.go:598-609): os.Stat(wtPath) -> gitutil.WorktreeRemoveForce -> os.RemoveAll(wtPath) fallback on remErr -> gitutil.WorktreePrune(g.Root), all best-effort, all before the BranchExists/DeleteBranch step that previously hard-failed with a raw 'checked out at <path>' git error. New test TestFinalizeEpic_OrphanedSpecBranch_LeftoverTempWorktreeSelfHeals recreates exactly the crashed-run shape (chore branch checked out in a worktree at the production wtPath) and asserts FinalizeEpic succeeds, the chore branch reaches the remote correctly, and the leftover worktree directory is gone afterward. Also checked the brief's path-traversal question directly: wtPath = workspace.FinalizeWorktreePath(root, cfg, specID), and specID must pass idvalidate.SpecID (rejects '/', '\\', '.', '..'; enforces `^[0-9]{3,}-[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$`) at internal/approve/impl.go:133 (ApproveImpl), the ONLY production caller of FinalizeEpic — confirmed via grep, no other call site exists outside tests/mocks. os.RemoveAll(wtPath) therefore cannot escape the worktrees directory; no traversal/collision risk.

TestFinalizeEpic_OrphanFetchFailureFallsBackToBaseline wires an origin bare repo with NO main ref ever pushed, so `git fetch origin main` genuinely fails inside FinalizeEpic. Asserts: err == nil (fallback, not a hard failure), stderr contains the 'could not fetch origin/main' warning, result.FinalizeBranch == '' , no chore/finalize branch exists on origin, and the baseline spec-branch push still landed. This closes exactly the gap I flagged in round 1 (my item 3) almost verbatim.

findings.item: NEW_ISSUE: RemoteHeadSHA silently returns the wrong SHA on ambiguous ls-remote matches
findings.detail: See concrete_changes_required #1. Empirically reproduced with real git (not speculative): `git ls-remote --heads origin chore/finalize-105` returns 2 lines when a decoy ref `refs/heads/aaa/chore/finalize-105` also exists, and the decoy's SHA sorts first. RemoteHeadSHA's `strings.Fields(strings.TrimSpace(out))[0]` silently picks whichever record is first, with no ambiguity check. Fail-safe in practice (PushBranchForceWithLease's lease targets the real fully-qualified ref regardless, so a wrong SHA just makes the push fail loudly rather than corrupt anything) and unreachable today given mindspec's specID format, but untested and worth a cheap, explicit exact-match guard.

findings.item: NEW_ISSUE: TOCTOU question answered narrowly by the lease; pre-existing human edits are NOT protected
findings.detail: Direct answer to the brief's TOCTOU question: a tip movement strictly BETWEEN the RemoteHeadSHA probe and the PushBranchForceWithLease call fails loudly, verified correct by design (git's compare-and-swap semantics) and by TestPushBranchForceWithLease_ArgvAndLease's stale-info case. But a human's commit already sitting on chore/finalize-<specID> from BEFORE the retry began (e.g. a review fixup pushed to the exact PR implApproveTail's NOTE instructs the operator to open on this branch) is not distinguished from 'a prior run's own push' — RemoteHeadSHA observes it correctly, the lease matches it exactly, and the force push overwrites it. The doc comments (gitops.go PushBranchForceWithLease and finalizeOrphanedSpecBranch's push-block comment) overclaim 'never silently clobbered' without this scoping. Not a regression and not blocking, but should be documented honestly, analogous to round 1's Group 5 squash-merge blind spot treatment.

A: driving run-1's failure via fake.removeErr injected into spec-worktree cleanup (after both pushes) is a faithful, well-justified crash shape and the resulting test (TestFinalizeEpic_OrphanedSpecBranch_RetryConverges) rigorously checks the retry replaces the remote tip with fresh content and stays a descendant of origin/main. B: the extra ordering test is a reasonable, correctly-scoped addition. C: the cwd save/restore via t.Cleanup in the composition test is correct Go test hygiene and the fix is properly scoped to the test file only.
